_description: ESPN team/player stats for Colombia (3 group games) and Ghana (3 group games), R32 COL vs GHA 2026-07-04

notes: Strong group (2W 1D, 7pts): beat Uzbekistan and Congo DR, held Portugal 0-0 despite 24 shots (finishing was the issue, not creation).
Very high shot volume team -- 24 shots vs Portugal is the biggest single-match shot count of any team we've tracked this campaign.
Offsides notably elevated: 3, 7(!), 3 -- the 7-offside game vs Congo DR is a real outlier but even excluding it their rate (3,3) is still moderate-high.
Goals: Daniel Muñoz (2, incl. winner vs CDR), Luis Díaz (1, + 1 assist), Jaminton Campaz (1). James Rodríguez: 0 goals, 0 assists in all 3 group games despite starting every game.

Advanced as a weaker qualifier (1W 1D 1L, 4pts). Extremely low attacking output -- 2 shots total vs England (21% possession, 24 fouls committed -- a purely defensive, foul-heavy survival performance).
Remarkably consistent low corner count: 2, 2, 2 across all 3 games regardless of opponent or game state.

Goals: Caleb Yirenkyi (stoppage-time winner vs Panama), Derrick Luckassen (vs Croatia). NEITHER Jordan Ayew NOR Antoine Semenyo has scored, assisted, or recorded a shot on target in any of the 3 group games.
Jordan Ayew: 0 shots (0.0) in ALL THREE games despite starting every game -- total attacking anonymity.
Antoine Semenyo: shots 2,0,1 (avg 1.0), but SOT is 0,0,0 -- zero shots on target across the entire group stage.
